agent_catalog.description: Produces structured natural-language summaries of ITSM records (incidents, requests, problems, changes) plus asset and CMDB CI contexts when applicable. Routing often aligns with conversational phrases such as 'summarize this ticket'; the canonical agent is selected by the planner using ticket id prefixes, UI context, and registry-grounded rules. For each (agent_id, service_id) pair, the executor's allowlisted tools and their invocation order are defined in agent-tool-mapping.json—typically: optional AI cache read, core ticket fields, timeline, cross-links, attachment metadata (names and ids only), synthesis, then cache write. Every tool in this agent's surface is read-only toward ITSM: they fetch data and manage an AI-side summary cache; they do not mutate ticket bodies or workflow state.

parameter_descriptions.service_id: ITSM module for parameterized summary tools (incident|request|problem|change); must match plan step.service_id
parameter_descriptions.ci_id: CMDB CI ID for CI summary
parameter_descriptions.tenant_id: Tenant scope identifier from runtime context
parameter_descriptions.include_related: List of related entities to include (e.g., ['asset', 'change'])
parameter_descriptions.user_id: Caller id for RBAC (often mirrored from orchestrator context)
parameter_descriptions.max_entries: Cap timeline entries returned by get_ticket_timeline
parameter_descriptions.max_items: Cap attachment metadata rows returned by get_ticket_attachment_metadata
parameter_descriptions.ticket_core: Pre-fetched core fields passed into summarize_entity (chained executor step)
parameter_descriptions.ticket_links: Pre-fetched linked CIs/tickets passed into summarize_entity (UC-1)
parameter_descriptions.attachment_metadata: Pre-fetched attachment metadata list passed into summarize_entity (UC-1)

examples.query: Give me ticket summary with asset info
UC-1 incident summary with links and attachment names only
Demo (no feature agent): user says summarize — planner should select summarization_agent, service_id incident, executor tool order per agent-tool-mapping

agent_catalog.personal: Clear, concise summarizer for ITSM records.
goals: Produce accurate summaries from ticket evidence
Keep user-facing output short and actionable
Preserve tenant and role context in summaries

agent_catalog.description: Surfaces semantically and metadata-aligned peer tickets for pattern discovery, duplicate awareness, and resolution reuse across incidents, requests, problems, and changes. Work is driven by the allowlisted find_similar_entities tool family (scoped by service_id in parameters). Typical inputs include ticket_id, tenant_id, and service_id; optional filters such as time window, status preference, similarity thresholds, and scope flags are defined on the tool entries in tool-registry.json. Results must respect tenant isolation and visibility—callers should not see candidates they cannot access in the source system.

parameter_descriptions.tenant_id: Tenant scope; results never cross tenants (UC-2.4)
parameter_descriptions.service_id: ITSM module for parameterized tool; must match plan step.service_id
parameter_descriptions.max_results: Max similar records to return (default: implementation-defined, cap in service)
parameter_descriptions.time_window_hours: Only consider tickets updated/created within this window (e.g. 168 = 7 days; UC-2.6)
parameter_descriptions.same_category_only: If true, restrict candidates to same category as source
parameter_descriptions.same_service_only: If true, restrict candidates to same affected service / service line as source when available
parameter_descriptions.prefer_status: Hint for ranking/post-filter: resolved | open | any
parameter_descriptions.min_similarity_score: Drop matches below this similarity (0.0-1.0; UC-2.3 low-signal case)
parameter_descriptions.include_related: Include related context in embedding/match text (CI, location, etc.)
parameter_descriptions.user_id: Caller id for RBAC filtering of candidate tickets

examples.query: Show me similar tickets from last 7 days
Similar resolved incidents in same category for resolution reuse

agent_catalog.personal: Similarity analyst focused on duplicate and pattern detection.
goals: Find semantically similar tickets with strong relevance
Highlight likely duplicates and reusable resolutions
Respect tenant boundaries and filters

agent_catalog.description: Retrieves ranked knowledge articles for chat questions, ticket-in-context scenarios, and explicit article opens. Three complementary paths are modeled: natural-language query search, ticket-grounded search (title, description, category signals), and fetch-by-id for a known article. Planner and executor bind to search_kb, search_kb_by_ticket, and get_kb_article as listed in agent-tool-mapping.json. Parameters usually include tenant_id and one of query, ticket_id, or kb_id, plus optional language, audience, publication filters, category constraints, and result limits as specified per tool in tool-registry.json.

parameter_descriptions.tenant_id: Tenant scope; KB data and ACLs are tenant-scoped (UC-3)
parameter_descriptions.query: Natural-language question for search_kb (UC-3 chat path)
parameter_descriptions.ticket_id: Ticket ID for search_kb_by_ticket — title/description/category as context (UC-3 UI button)
parameter_descriptions.kb_id: KB article id for get_kb_article (same as tool-registry; replaces legacy kb_id name)
parameter_descriptions.top_k: Max search hits to return (maps to tool-registry top_k)
parameter_descriptions.language: BCP-47 locale; prefer articles in this language, fallback in service (UC-3.7)
parameter_descriptions.audience: end_user | technician | any — filters technician-only articles for end users (UC-3.6)
parameter_descriptions.published_only: If true (default when omitted in implementation), exclude draft (UC-3.4)
parameter_descriptions.exclude_archived: If true (default), exclude archived articles from search
parameter_descriptions.category_filter: Restrict search to a KB category when set
parameter_descriptions.user_id: Caller id for RBAC on article visibility

examples.query: Search KB for VPN reset instructions

agent_catalog.personal: Knowledge retrieval specialist for fast article discovery.
goals: Return the most relevant KB articles for user intent
Prefer published and audience-appropriate content
Cite concise rationale for selected results

agent_catalog.description: Infers end-user sentiment (e.g. frustration, satisfaction, neutrality) from ticket narrative and, when provided, conversation thread context—suited to agent-facing badges, escalation hints, and quality analytics. Scope is incidents and requests only (read path). Execution centers on analyze_entity_sentiment; inputs routinely include ticket_id, tenant_id, and service_id. Optional controls include analysis_scope (ticket-wide vs single comment vs full thread trajectory), comment identifiers or text for triggered analysis, caps on customer comments for long threads, locale or language hints, and whether to include non-customer-authored content.

parameter_descriptions.service_id: incident|request; must match plan step.service_id and tool supported_service_ids
parameter_descriptions.comment_id: For per-comment / auto-trigger path when known
parameter_descriptions.comment_text: Inline customer text when analyzing without persisting fetch
parameter_descriptions.max_customer_comments: Cap customer comments for trajectory on high-volume tickets (UC-4.8)
parameter_descriptions.language: BCP-47 hint; analyze in source language, labels may normalize to English (UC-4.7)
parameter_descriptions.include_non_customer: If true, include agent notes (default false — skip work notes UC-4.4)

examples.query: Is the customer frustrated about REQ123?

agent_catalog.personal: Conversation sentiment analyst for customer tone and escalation risk.
goals: Assess sentiment from conversation and ticket context
Surface escalation risk early with confidence
Differentiate customer vs internal signals

agent_catalog.description: Automates classification, priority assessment, near-duplicate detection against recent work, and assignment recommendations for incidents and requests. The ordered tool set (classify_entity, prioritize_entity, check_duplicate_candidates, assign_entity) and per-service rows live in agent-tool-mapping.json. Supports both advisory mode—returning recommendations without persisting—and explicit application of assignment when policy and user consent allow. service_id must be incident or request; mixed read and action operation types reflect pre-check tools before mutating assignment state.

parameter_descriptions.service_id: incident|request; must match tool supported_service_ids and mapping row
parameter_descriptions.re_triage: True when re-running pipeline after enriched details (UC-5.7)
parameter_descriptions.recommendation_only: Chat path: classify/prioritize propose without persisting (UC-5.11)
parameter_descriptions.apply_assignment: False to return assign recommendation only; true to persist (assign_entity)
parameter_descriptions.title_snippet: Short title text for classification when not fetched yet
parameter_descriptions.affected_ci_hint: CI string from description for routing enrichment (UC-5.5)
parameter_descriptions.sentiment_hint: Optional sentiment signal for urgency (UC-5 pipeline)
parameter_descriptions.time_window_hours: Duplicate scan lookback; default 48 in implementation (UC-5 Step 3)
parameter_descriptions.actor_user_id: Actor for request-type or audit (optional on read-only triage steps)
parameter_descriptions.routing_confidence: Combined confidence for auto vs triage-queue policy
parameter_descriptions.duplicate_of_ticket_id: When duplicate found, link before assign (UC-5.4)

agent_catalog.personal: Operational triage specialist for routing and prioritization.
goals: Classify and prioritize tickets consistently
Suggest best-fit assignment paths
Identify duplicate candidates before assignment

agent_catalog.description: Performs state-changing and communicative work on existing incidents and requests: lifecycle transitions (close, reopen, resolve, cancel), threaded comments (customer-visible or internal work notes), sparse field patches that avoid implicit state jumps (priority, title, description, category, urgency, and similar scalar or small-struct updates), and relationship edges including duplicate, related, parent, and child links with optional cross-service targeting via target_service_id. Entirely action-typed toward ITSM; every invocation must carry a consistent service_id (incident or request) and respect RBAC and approval policy in the integrating platform.

parameter_descriptions.actor_user_id: Actor for audit and ITSM mutation
parameter_descriptions.service_id: incident|request; must match tool supported_service_ids and mapping row
parameter_descriptions.lifecycle_tool_id: Exactly one allowlisted tool_id: close_entity | reopen_entity | resolve_entity | cancel_entity (see agent-tool-mapping)

agent_catalog.personal: Lifecycle action specialist for safe ticket state changes.
goals: Execute lifecycle actions with policy-safe transitions
Keep updates and links precise and auditable
Avoid unsafe or ambiguous mutations

agent_catalog.description: Creates new incidents or service requests through guided slot capture while reducing duplicate noise: a read-style duplicate or open-ticket check typically precedes create_ticket, in the order defined in agent-tool-mapping.json. Supports human-in-the-loop flows via preview-only drafts and explicit user confirmation before submit, on-behalf creation using affected_user_id, and resumption of in-progress forms through a partial draft identifier. service_id distinguishes incident versus request creation; tool parameters for mandatory slots and optional enrichments are authoritative in tool-registry.json.

parameter_descriptions.title: Ticket title (required for create_ticket)
parameter_descriptions.caller_user_id: Authenticated user (reporter for incident, requester for request); maps to tool create_ticket
parameter_descriptions.affected_user_id: On-behalf / affected user when not caller (UC-6.8)
parameter_descriptions.ci_display_name: Human-readable CI label for description enrichment
parameter_descriptions.use_defaults_for_missing: True for just create it with defaults (UC-6.5)
parameter_descriptions.preview_only: True returns draft payload without persisting ticket
parameter_descriptions.confirmed_by_user: True after user confirms preview (UC-6 turn 7-8)

inputs.title: VPN connection failed
inputs.description: User cannot connect to VPN after password reset

examples.query: Submit after preview — make it P2

inputs.title: VPN connection failed
inputs.description: User cannot connect to VPN after password reset

examples.query: Ticket for my manager on laptop issue

agent_catalog.personal: Creation assistant for structured, complete new tickets.
goals: Collect required details with minimal friction
Prevent duplicate submissions before create
Produce clean ticket drafts and confirmed creates

agent_catalog.description: Proposes ranked resolution hypotheses from similar resolved work, knowledge, and known-error signals, then optionally records whether the user found a given suggestion helpful. suggest_resolution is a read step; record_feedback is an action that stores structured feedback for future ranking. Both require service_id incident or request. Read path parameters often include filters to exclude steps already reflected in work notes, toggles for which evidence sources to include, a lookback window for precedent search, and formatting suitable for chat versus structured UI.

parameter_descriptions.include_similar_resolved: Search similar resolved tickets (default true in impl)
parameter_descriptions.filter_already_tried_from_work_notes: Mark or de-prioritize steps seen in work notes (UC-7.2)
parameter_descriptions.conversational_format: Shape output for chat vs UI list (UC-7.5)

agent_catalog.personal: Resolution advisor for practical, evidence-backed fixes.
goals: Suggest actionable resolutions ranked by likely effectiveness
Incorporate prior ticket and KB evidence
Capture feedback to improve future suggestions

agent_catalog.description: Orchestrates catalog-driven and onboarding-style fulfillment: deduplication checks against in-flight work, status and task-tree inspection, workflow execution or simulation, and granular task updates when technicians complete manual steps. Applies to service_id values catalog, request, or onboarding according to the mapping rows in agent-tool-mapping.json. Flags commonly distinguish plan-only decomposition from execution, template or approval gates, and SLA or exception paths. update_fulfillment_task marks specific fulfillment tasks complete (e.g. technician checklist items), which may unblock dependent tasks or downstream automation in the fulfillment engine.

parameter_descriptions.actor_user_id: Actor for execute and sensitive reads
parameter_descriptions.subject_key: Dedupe key for check_fulfillment_duplicate (e.g. employee email)
parameter_descriptions.workflow_inputs: Slot data: employee name, start date, department, etc.

examples.query: Onboard John Smith March 15 — plan only

agent_catalog.personal: Workflow fulfillment orchestrator for request execution.
goals: Drive fulfillment workflows with clear status updates
Handle duplicate checks and execution paths safely
Support manual task updates and dependency progression
